jokes.punchline: Hank immediately repeats the crude comment after learning it's Larry's girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Hank's oblivious social skills create genuine cringe. The callback structure works well and it's very economical.

jokes.text: That's 'cause I'm Not chokin' up as much On my penis.
jokes.setup: Artie compliments Larry's recent performance on the show
jokes.punchline: Larry attributes his success to a bizarre sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry Sanders inappropriate overshare - perfectly in character but crude humor brings it down slightly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's 'cause I'm Not chokin' up as much On my penis.

jokes.text: I've been Feeling something Lately. I couldn't put my finger On it. Maybe it's that-- What'd you call it? Happy!
jokes.setup: Larry is told he seems happy lately
jokes.punchline: Larry can't even identify the emotion of happiness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Larry's complete emotional disconnection. Perfect integration of his neuroses.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I've been Feeling something Lately. I couldn't put my finger On it. Maybe it's that-- What'd you call it? Happy!

jokes.text: Hey, do you think When I come out To do the monologue, I should just rush To the front of the stage And shake everyone's hand The way Leno does?
jokes.setup: Larry is having a good moment with Alex
jokes.punchline: He immediately shifts to obsessing about copying his competition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's insecurity and competitive nature interrupting a personal moment.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hey, do you think When I come out To do the monologue, I should just rush To the front of the stage And shake everyone's hand The way Leno does?

jokes.text: Yeah, he's still Got to take Hank apart And put him back In the box.
jokes.setup: Artie explains he can't come to dinner because work isn't done
jokes.punchline: Larry describes Hank as a toy that needs to be stored away
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures the dynamic between producer and talent. Original and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, he's still Got to take Hank apart And put him back In the box.

jokes.text: Why, are you quitting?
jokes.setup: Hank says Larry is lucky to have Alex
jokes.punchline: Larry deflects the compliment with self-deprecating workplace humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - Larry can't take a compliment without making it about work anxieties.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Why, are you quitting?

jokes.text: I don't usually hear that Until my pants are off.
jokes.setup: Larry tells Alex to put something away
jokes.punchline: Alex makes an inappropriate sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual innuendo - gets a laugh but lacks originality and character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I don't usually hear that Until my pants are off.

jokes.text: A man dressing as a woman Is a little...Benny hill. Don't you think? That's what we are. We arebenny hill.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests the mermaid sketch is too broad with a man in drag
jokes.punchline: Larry has a horrified realization about his show's comedy level
jokes.explanation: Great meta-comedy moment about comedy itself. Larry's existential crisis about his show's sophistication.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: A man dressing as a woman Is a little...Benny hill. Don't you think?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's what we are. We arebenny hill.

jokes.text: You know, I could play that part. Of the, uh... I'm sorry. Oh, god. Of the mermaid or-- That was so just... Presumptuous.
jokes.setup: Alex is critiquing the show's comedy
jokes.punchline: She immediately reveals her real motivation by asking for the part
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the calculating nature behind Alex's 'helpful' criticism. Realistic awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You know, I could play that part. Of the, uh... I'm sorry. Oh, god. Of the mermaid or-- That was so just... Presumptuous.

jokes.text: Are you talking To me or my ass?
jokes.setup: Hank enters calling Larry 'o great one'
jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan response about who Hank is addressing
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery and economy, typical Larry deflection of praise with crude humor.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Are you talking To me or my ass?

jokes.text: No, I mean He stopped drinking Because he fell Into lake michigan and drowned. Oh, god. Which of The 12 steps is that? The last one.
jokes.setup: Discussion about someone getting their drinking under control
jokes.punchline: Reveals the person died, then makes a dark 12-step program joke
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - perfect structure building to the death reveal, then Artie's perfectly timed deadpan follow-up. Classic Larry Sanders darkness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No, I mean He stopped drinking Because he fell Into lake michigan and drowned.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, god. Which of The 12 steps is that?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The last one.

jokes.text: That would be Her brain, Hank, An organ that you may not Be familiar with.
jokes.setup: Hank claims there's something behind Alex's eyes
jokes.punchline: Artie delivers a brutal intellectual insult to Hank
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie putdown with good structure. Perfect character voice and relationship dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That would be Her brain, Hank, An organ that you may not Be familiar with.

jokes.text: Tonight the mermaid Will be played By a lovely and talented Lady instead of me.
jokes.setup: Hank introduces the mermaid sketch on air
jokes.punchline: He breaks character to complain about being replaced
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - his ego and bitterness bleeding through into the actual show. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Tonight the mermaid Will be played By a lovely and talented Lady instead of me.

jokes.text: Cover your blow-hole.
jokes.setup: Discussion about marrying an octopus with 8 arms
jokes.punchline: Whale anatomy used as sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Decent pun within the sketch context but fairly predictable sexual humor. Gets the job done.

dialogue.line: Cover your blow-hole.

jokes.text: Well, I am the mermaid. Does Larry come back After this?
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Hank isn't playing the mermaid
jokes.punchline: Hank's delusional response followed by someone wanting to escape
jokes.explanation: Good Hank character moment showing his delusion, followed by natural reaction from uncomfortable observer.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, I am the mermaid.
Does Larry come back After this?

jokes.text: I mean, do you really Buy that she has ever Been underwater? I don't. I'm sorry, I don't.
jokes.setup: Hank is critiquing Alex's mermaid performance
jokes.punchline: His absurdly literal criticism of mermaid authenticity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank logic - taking sketch comedy literally to justify his bitterness. Great character-specific absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I mean, do you really Buy that she has ever Been underwater? I don't. I'm sorry, I don't.

jokes.text: They're so lucky, And you don't hear People standing around Saying, 'look how fat That whale got.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about whales eating anything
jokes.punchline: Observation about double standards for whale weight
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor but feels more like generic stand-up than character-specific comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: They're so lucky, And you don't hear People standing around Saying, 'look how fat That whale got.'

jokes.text: I wanted It tomorrow so I could Give it to my agent.
jokes.setup: Alex asks for a videocassette of the show
jokes.punchline: Reveals her calculating career motivations
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Alex's opportunistic nature, but fairly predictable in this context.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I wanted It tomorrow so I could Give it to my agent.

jokes.text: That was my idea. It's still good.
jokes.setup: Larry claims credit for Leno's opening routine
jokes.punchline: Warren's diplomatic but dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good interaction showing Larry's insecurity and Warren's polite dismissal. Economical and character-driven.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That was my idea. It's still good.

jokes.text: We've got a thing Called must pee tv.
jokes.setup: NBC's 'Must See TV' slogan
jokes.punchline: HBO's crude parody version

jokes.text: Larry and I are very, Very close. Larry. You understand? That's a comfort That comes From years and years Of working together.
jokes.setup: Artie's talking head confessional about his relationship with Larry
jokes.punchline: The awkward pause and emphasis suggesting deeper meaning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment suggesting Artie's paternal/romantic feelings for Larry through subtext.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Larry and I are very, Very close. Larry. You understand? That's a comfort That comes From years and years Of working together.

jokes.punchline: Her obvious lie met with Artie's sarcastic response

dialogue.speaker: Alex

jokes.text: That's because they have A lot of twisters there. You got shit flying around. You're not gonna be watching tv.

jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd explanation blaming tornadoes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic that's somehow almost reasonable. Original and memorable, classic Larry rationalization.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's because they have A lot of twisters there. You got shit flying around. You're not gonna be watching tv.

jokes.text: Slap on Your shelled bra, Little mermaid. You're back in business.
jokes.setup: Hank has been restored to the mermaid role
jokes.punchline: Artie's crude but affectionate way of delivering good news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie voice - crude but caring, with great specificity. 'Shelled bra' is a memorable detail.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Slap on Your shelled bra, Little mermaid. You're back in business.

jokes.text: I happen to be In love with you. And, uh... I'm confused.
jokes.setup: Alex asking if Larry cares about her career
jokes.punchline: Larry's vulnerable admission immediately followed by confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - even his romantic declarations are undermined by neurotic confusion. Great character work.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I happen to be In love with you. And, uh... I'm confused.

jokes.text: I happen to be In love with you. And, uh... I'm confused.
jokes.setup: Larry and Alex are having relationship tension
jokes.punchline: Larry's emotionally stunted declaration of love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry emotional moment - can't express love without immediately undercutting it with confusion. Great character work.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I happen to be In love with you. And, uh... I'm confused.

dialogue.line: Don't speak or you're fish Will get very cold.
